channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Interviewing Donald Trump: A last-minute blitz and new closing message
item.description: I just interviewed former President Trump once more – and what I saw was a candidate with a clear closing message who still appears slightly too eager to deviate from his script.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump stops by primetime NFL matchup between the Jets and Steelers to cap dizzying weekend
item.description: Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump attended Sunday night's NFL game between the Pittsburgh Steelers and New York Jets as the guest of an unidentified suite holder at Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raffensperger denies voter fraud amid Georgia’s historic early-vote turnout
item.description: “We’re going to respond quickly to these sorts of things in 2024 because it’s not supported by the facts,” Raffensperger said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: 11 million illegals would have become citizens under bill that Kamala Harris promoted
item.description: A bill touted by Vice President Kamala Harris in her interview with Fox News' Bret Baier would have paved a path to citizenship for an estimated 11 million illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ‘insulting’ Harris ad targets Black men's love lives
item.description: A new advertisement from the Harris campaign depicts a Black man being rejected on a fictional dating show after he tells potentially interested suitors that he isn't voting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk's daily $1 million payouts at Trump rally draw legal scrutiny
item.description: "By the way, John had no idea. So anyway, you're welcome," the Tesla founder said as he handed Dreher the check.
item.dc:creator: Jarrett Renshaw / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Harris invokes Jimmy Carter in bid to get supporters to vote early
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris told supporters in Atlanta, Georgia, Saturday that former President Jimmy Carter had already cast his ballot in the election and they can too.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Ohio Gov. Bob Taft, scion of Republican family, backs Democrat Sherrod Brown for Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Former Ohio Gov. Bob Taft, scion of one of the state's best-known Republican families, threw his support Sunday behind Democratic Sen. Sherrod Brown in his hotly contested reelection race against GOP nominee Bernie Moreno.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents that assess Israel's plans to attack Iran, three U.S. officials told The Associated Press. A fourth U.S. official said the documents appear to be legitimate.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, MIKE BALSAMO, ERIC TUCKER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans and Democrats are both preparing for long legal battles over the 2024 election results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Donald Trump, who still refuses to accept that he lost the 2020 election to Democrat Joe Biden, says he wants a presidential victory Nov. 5 to be so overwhelming that the results are “too big to rig.” “We want a landslide," he recently told supporters in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, COLLEEN LONG and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' as she spotlights abortion restrictions in Georgia during early voting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said Saturday that Republican former President Donald Trump was “cruel” for how he talked about the grieving family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting 20 hours for a hospital to treat her complications from an abortion pill, as she put combating re
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris makes last-ditch appeal to disenchanted Republicans
item.description: Vice President Harris and her allies are making a last-ditch effort to peel off Republican support from former President Trump in the final days of the election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Five takeaways from a fiery Wisconsin Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) and Republican challenger Eric Hovde squared off on a debate stage Friday evening in Madison in a fiery exchange of attacks that showed how intense this race has become after largely flying under the radar for months.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: Chauncey Parker talks new role as NYC deputy mayor for public safety
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Chauncey Parker to be New York City’s new deputy mayor for public safety. Parker takes over the position that was held by Philip Banks, who resigned the week prior.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Texas Republicans demand answers about Venezuelan gang members
item.description: White House spokesperson Angelo Fernández Hernández has called reports Biden is "secretly flying" people into the U.S. “categorically false.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: SBA administrator visits Tampa Bay as disaster loan program runs dry
item.description: While visiting businesses in Tampa Bay, which included both of those businesses, SBA Administrator Isabel Casillas Guzman said the next move is up to members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Question 3: 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide
item.description: Dr. Sandra Cosgrove and Kerry Durmick joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to provide a proponent and opponent's view of one of the most contentious issues on the Nevada ballot in November: Question 3.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
